originalTitle: Toddlers among more than 50 schoolchildren kidnapped in Nigeria
title: Sad News from Nigeria: Children are Missing, But We Hope They Come Home Soon
content:
Hello, my wonderful friends all across Africa! Your friendly news reporter is here. Today, I have some news that might make us feel a little sad, but it's important to talk about it. We are going to learn about a town called Mussa, far away in a country called Nigeria. Let's send our kind thoughts to them.

What Happened in Mussa Town?

Imagine going to school, playing with your friends, and learning new things. That's what children in Mussa town love to do too! But recently, something very sad happened there. Some children, just like you, went to school. Then, they went missing. We don't know exactly why, but some people took them away from their school. This made their families and the whole town very, very worried and sad. More than 50 schoolchildren are missing. Can you imagine? That's a lot of friends, brothers, and sisters! Some of these children are very small, like little toddlers. A toddler is a child who is just learning to walk and talk, maybe only two or three years old. They should be playing with their toys and being safe at home with their parents.

Where is Mussa Town?

Let's find Mussa on our big map of Africa! Nigeria is a big country in West Africa, with many people and many different towns and villages. Mussa town is in a part of Nigeria called Borno State. Borno State is a beautiful place, but sometimes, bad things happen there. Like in any place in the world, people hope for peace and safety for everyone. Did you know? Nigeria is the country with the most people in all of Africa! It has many different languages and cultures, making it a very special place.

Why is School So Important?

We all know school is super important, right? It's where we learn to read, write, count, and make new friends. It's where we learn about the world and grow smarter every day. For children in Mussa, school is just as important. Going to school helps them dream big dreams for their future and helps their community grow stronger. When children can't go to school, or when something like this happens, it makes everyone sad because it stops them from learning and playing. People everywhere believe that every child deserves to be safe at school and at home.

How Are People Feeling in Mussa?

The people in Mussa town are feeling very sad and worried. When someone you love goes missing, especially a child, it's a very difficult time. Parents are very sad and are praying for their children to come back home safely. Their hearts ache for their little ones. The whole town feels like a part of their family is missing. But even when they are sad, the people in Mussa are also very strong. They are holding hands and helping each other. They are talking to each other, praying together, and hoping for good news. This is what we call 'community spirit' – when everyone comes together to support each other in tough times.

What Are Grown-ups Doing to Help?

Many grown-ups are working very hard to find the missing children. The parents, town leaders, police, and even soldiers are all trying their best. They are looking everywhere and asking questions. They want to know what happened and, most importantly, they want to bring all the children back home to their families. Did you know? There are special people called aid workers who help communities when bad things happen. They bring food, water, and comfort to people who need it most. No one has said yet, "We did this." We don't know who took the children or why. But the important thing is that many, many people are looking and trying to make things right again. Everyone hopes the children will be safe and sound very soon.

How Can We Help?

Even though we might be far away, we can still help in our hearts. We can think good thoughts for the children and their families in Mussa. We can talk to our parents about what happened and learn more about staying safe in our own communities. We can also be extra kind to our friends and family. Let's remember how important it is to be safe and to always tell a trusted grown-up if we see something that doesn't feel right. Let's also be grateful for our schools and teachers.

Let's Keep Hoping and Being Strong

This news from Mussa town reminds us that sometimes bad things happen, but it also reminds us how important it is for everyone to work together. We all want a safe Africa where children can play, learn, and grow up happily. Your reporter knows that the people of Mussa are strong, and we hope with all our hearts that the children will be back home very, very soon. Let's keep them in our thoughts and send them lots of love.
summary: More than 50 schoolchildren, including toddlers, went missing from their school in Mussa town, Nigeria, making their families and the whole community very sad, but many grown-ups are working hard to find them and bring them home safely.
whyMatters: This news matters because every child deserves to be safe, especially at school, and it shows us how important it is for communities to stick together and for grown-ups to work hard to protect all children.

originalTitle: Senior IS leader killed in joint operation, US and Nigeria say
title: Senior IS leader killed in joint operation, US and Nigeria say
content: President Donald Trump said the operation eliminated "the most active terrorist in the world", Abu-Bilal al-Minuki.

originalTitle: New outbreak of Ebola kills 80 in eastern DR Congo
title: New outbreak of Ebola kills 80 in eastern DR Congo
content: Africa's top health agency says around 246 cases have been reported - a case has also been reported in Uganda.

content: British capital sees heightened security as right wing rally takes place at the same time as Nakba Day march.

title: Zimbabwe’s diaspora reshapes real estate and farming investment trends
content: Zimbabwean digital influencers appear to influence diaspora investment decisions through social media.

originalTitle: Mexico teachers threaten World Cup disruption over pay
title: Mexico teachers threaten World Cup disruption over pay
content: Mexico’s teachers are threatening to disrupt next month’s 2026 FIFA World Cup if their demands for higher pay.

originalTitle: Rescue diver dies during search for bodies of Italians who drowned in Maldives caves
title: Rescue diver dies during search for bodies of Italians who drowned in Maldives caves
content: Staff Sgt Mohamed Mahdhee died while diving in the search-and-rescue operation, the Maldives government tells the BBC.

originalTitle: US planning to charge ex-Cuban leader Raúl Castro
title: US planning to charge ex-Cuban leader Raúl Castro
content: Charges could come as soon as next week in a case reportedly focused on Cuba's downing of two planes in 1996.

originalTitle: Kerr equals record in final game as Chelsea beat Man Utd
title: Kerr equals record in final game as Chelsea beat Man Utd
content: Sam Kerr becomes Chelsea's joint all-time top goalscorer alongside Fran Kirby, as her 116th club goal on her final appearance gives Sonia Bompastor's side a 1-0 win over Manchester United at Stamford Bridge.

originalTitle: Man Utd reach agreement to appoint Carrick manager
title: Man Utd reach agreement to appoint Carrick manager
content: Manchester United reach an agreement with Michael Carrick to become the club's permanent head coach.

originalTitle: Aston Villa secure Champions League spot with win over Liverpool
title: Aston Villa secure Champions League spot with win over Liverpool
content: Ollie Watkins scores twice as Aston Villa move above Liverpool into fourth in the Premier League and seal qualification for next season's Champions League.
